definition: Average True Range (absolute value), representing volatility/risk.
interpretation: Used for stop-loss (e.g. 2×ATR) and position sizing; does not indicate direction.

definition: MA as the middle band, standard deviation as the bandwidth.
interpretation: Narrowing bands (squeeze) signal an impending move; price riding the upper band = strength.

definition: Weighted moving average that weights recent prices more heavily, more responsive than SMA.
interpretation: Used like SMA but reacts faster with less lag; commonly 12/26-day.

definition: Difference between two EMAs, measuring trend direction, strength and momentum shifts.
formula: DIF=EMA(C,12)−EMA(C,26); DEA=EMA(DIF,9); Histogram=(DIF−DEA)×2 (×2 is the Chinese TongDaXin/THS convention; international platforms StockCharts/TradingView do not multiply by 2, i.e. DIF−DEA)
interpretation: DIF crossing above DEA = golden cross (buy), crossing below = death cross (sell); new price high while DIF does not = top divergence (bearish).

definition: Measures the relative strength of upward vs downward moves.
formula: RS = mean gain / mean loss (Wilder smoothing); RSI = 100 − 100/(1+RS); period n=14
interpretation: >70 overbought, <30 oversold; 50 is the bull/bear divide; new high unconfirmed = top divergence.

definition: Arithmetic mean of the last n closes, smoothing price and identifying trend direction.
interpretation: Price above the MA is bullish, below is bearish; commonly 5/10/20/60/120/250-day; short MA crossing above long MA = golden cross (buy), crossing below = death cross (sell).

definition: Measures overbought/oversold by the close's position within the recent high-low range.
formula: RSV=(C−n-low)/(n-high−n-low)×100, K=⅔Kₚᵣₑᵥ+⅓RSV, D=⅔Dₚᵣₑᵥ+⅓K, J=3K−2D; international Stochastic convention: %K=RSV, %D=SMA(%K,3), Slow/Full versions smooth once more (using SMA rather than the recursion above)
interpretation: >80 overbought, <20 oversold; K crossing above D = golden cross; J overshooting the bounds signals reversal.

definition: The most basic volume data.
interpretation: Rising on expanding volume = healthy; rising on shrinking volume = weak momentum; breakouts require volume.

definition: Volume-weighted average traded price, an institutional cost benchmark.
interpretation: Price above VWAP is bullish; acts as intraday support/resistance.

definition: Relative band width, quantifying volatility and squeezes.
interpretation: Historical lows = squeeze (often precedes a big move); sharp expansion = breakout.

definition: Measures how far price deviates from its statistical mean.
interpretation: >+100 strong/overbought, <−100 weak/oversold.

name_zh: 动向指标/平均趋向指数
definition: DMI gauges direction, ADX gauges trend strength (directionless).
interpretation: +DI crossing above −DI = buy; ADX>25 strong trend, <20 ranging.

definition: Uses Fibonacci ratios to mark potential support/resistance within a pullback.
interpretation: Pullbacks to 0.382/0.5/0.618 often find support; 0.618 draws the most attention.

definition: Price vacuum between adjacent candles, reflecting abrupt sentiment shifts.
formula: Gap up: today's L>yesterday's H; gap down: today's H<yesterday's L
interpretation: Breakaway gaps confirm a trend and rarely fill; exhaustion gaps signal the end; most common gaps get filled.

definition: Displays support/resistance, trend and momentum in a single chart.
formula: Conversion Line=(9-high+9-low)/2; Base Line=(26-high+26-low)/2; Leading Span A=(Conversion+Base)/2 shifted forward 26; Leading Span B=(52-high+52-low)/2 shifted forward 26; Lagging Span=C shifted back 26
interpretation: Price above the cloud is bullish, below is bearish; Conversion crossing above Base = buy; a thick cloud = strong support/resistance.

definition: Examples: Hammer/Hanging Man (long lower shadow), Inverted Hammer/Shooting Star (long upper shadow), Bullish/Bearish Engulfing (later body engulfs prior), Doji (open≈close), Dark Cloud Cover/Piercing, Harami.
formula: pattern matching on relative OHLC sizes and body/shadow ratios (CDLHAMMER, CDLENGULFING)
interpretation: Requires confirmation by top/bottom location and trend; more reliable on expanding volume.

definition: Examples: Morning Star (bottom, bullish)/Evening Star (top, bearish), Three White Soldiers/Three Black Crows, Three Inside Up/Down, Three Outside Up/Down.
formula: matching the direction, size and overlap of three candles (CDLMORNINGSTAR, CDL3WHITESOLDIERS)
interpretation: More reliable than single candles, especially at key price levels.

definition: Volume-weighted momentum oscillator (RSI-style, incorporating volume).
formula: MF=HLC3×V split into positive/negative, MFI=100−100/(1+positive MF/negative MF), n=14
interpretation: >80 overbought, <20 oversold; more reliable with volume.

definition: Uses cumulative volume direction to measure net buying/selling inflow.
formula: Up day OBV+V, down day OBV−V
interpretation: Moving with price = volume-price confirmation; price up while OBV does not = divergence (warning).

definition: Intraday support/resistance computed from the prior period's OHLC, common intraday.
interpretation: Price above PP is bullish; breaks of R/S often accelerate.

definition: ATR-based trend-following line giving clear long/short and stop-loss levels.
interpretation: Price above the line = long (green), below = short (red); a flip is the signal, usable as a trailing stop.

definition: Applies the Stochastic to RSI, making it more sensitive.
formula: StochRSI=(RSI−n-period RSI low)/(n-period RSI high−n-period RSI low)
interpretation: >80 overbought, <20 oversold; earlier signals but more false ones.

definition: Lines connecting highs/lows depict direction and slope, copied in parallel to form a channel.
formula: Fit ≥2 lows (uptrend) or highs (downtrend), channel=trend line+parallel line
interpretation: A valid break below/above = possible trend reversal; sell high and buy low within the channel.

definition: Examples: Head & Shoulders Top/Bottom (neckline break, target≈head-to-neckline height), Double Top (M)/Double Bottom (W), Triple Top/Bottom, Rounding Top/Bottom, V-shape.
formula: peak-trough geometry + neckline break; target=projected pattern height
interpretation: Neckline break + volume = confirmation; trade failed patterns in reverse.

definition: Examples: Triangles (symmetrical/ascending/descending), Flag/Pennant (target≈flagpole height), Wedge, Rectangle, Cup & Handle.
formula: converging/parallel trend lines and break direction; target measured from pattern height
interpretation: Volume shrinks during consolidation and expands on the break; breakouts in the original trend direction have a higher win rate.

definition: Combines the close's position within the range with volume to gauge accumulation/distribution.
interpretation: Rising = capital inflow; price up while ADL falls = distribution divergence.

definition: Three smoothed MAs (Jaw/Teeth/Lips) to judge whether the trend is sleeping or feeding.
formula: Jaw=SMMA(HL2,13) shifted forward 8; Teeth=SMMA(HL2,8) shifted forward 5; Lips=SMMA(HL2,5) shifted forward 3
interpretation: Intertwined = sleeping (ranging); opening up with Lips>Teeth>Jaw = uptrend.

definition: Gaussian-weighted windowed MA balancing low lag and smoothness.
interpretation: Larger offset = more sensitive; crossovers/turns serve as signals.

definition: Three parallel tracks drawn from three pivot points.
formula: Median line=A pointing to the midpoint of BC; upper/lower tines pass through B, C and parallel the median
interpretation: Touching the median often reverts; touching the outer tines often reverses.

definition: Uses the fast/slow MA difference of the median price to measure market momentum.
interpretation: Crossing above zero = momentum turns bullish; "saucer" and "twin peaks" are classic buy/sell signals; commonly paired with Alligator/Fractals.

definition: Measures the number of periods since the last high/low to judge trend freshness.
formula: Up=(n−periods since the n-period high)/n×100, Down likewise, n=25; Oscillator=Up−Down
interpretation: Up>70 and Down<30 = strong uptrend; Up crossing above Down = bullish.

definition: Price's relative position within the bands (0=lower band, 1=upper band).
interpretation: >1 breaks above the upper band (overbought), <0 breaks below the lower band (oversold).

definition: Separately measures bull and bear power relative to the EMA.
interpretation: In an uptrend, Bear<0 but recovering = buy point; divergence warns of reversal.

definition: Measures how far price deviates from its linear-regression forecast value.
interpretation: >0 stronger, <0 weaker; sustained deviation signals trend strength or mean-reversion pressure.

definition: The momentum version of the A/D Line (accumulation/distribution) — the fast/slow MA difference of volume money flow.
interpretation: Crossing above zero = money-flow momentum turns positive (bullish); divergence with price warns; completes the A/D Line—CMF—Chaikin Oscillator volume trio.

definition: Measures "trend vs ranging" (directionless).
interpretation: >61.8 ranging, <38.2 strong trend.

definition: Uses the rate of change in the high-low spread to measure volatility expansion/contraction.
formula: (EMA(H−L,10)−its value n periods ago)/its value n periods ago×100
interpretation: A sharp rise = surging volatility (top panic/breakout); a slow decline = settling into calm.

definition: A range-bound version of A/D.
interpretation: >0 buying pressure dominates, <0 selling pressure dominates; >0.05 bullish, <−0.05 bearish.

definition: Uses the difference between up and down momentum to measure pure momentum.
interpretation: −100~+100; +50 overbought, −50 oversold.

definition: A long-term (monthly) bottom-signal indicator.
interpretation: Turning up from below the zero line = long-term buy; used for index timing.

definition: A short-term mean-reversion indicator combining "price RSI + streak RSI + percent-rank of returns".
interpretation: <10 extremely oversold (buy), >90 extremely overbought (sell).

definition: A channel formed by recent highs and lows, the core of the Turtle method.
interpretation: A new n-period high = buy, a new low = sell.

definition: A combination of multiple EMAs to offset lag.
interpretation: Lag markedly lower than a plain EMA, suited to fast trend-following.

definition: Price fluctuation with the trend removed, used to identify cycle length.
interpretation: Peak-to-trough spacing estimates the cycle; does not reflect the current trend.

definition: An adaptive version of RSI that changes its lookback period by volatility.
formula: n_dynamic=14/(recent σ/long-term σ), then compute RSI
interpretation: Used like RSI, reacts faster in high volatility.

definition: A channel set a fixed percentage above and below a MA.
interpretation: Touching the upper band is overbought, touching the lower band is oversold.

definition: Measures the "ease" of a price rise/fall.
interpretation: >0 and rising = easy advance; near 0 = stalling on high volume.

definition: Combines the direction and magnitude of price change with volume to measure bull/bear power.
interpretation: Crossing above zero = buyers dominate; divergence warns of reversal.

definition: Projects target levels after a breakout.
interpretation: 1.618 and 2.618 are common take-profit/resistance targets.

definition: "Gaussianizes" the price distribution to amplify turning points.
formula: Fisher=½ln((1+x)/(1−x)), x is the normalized price position
interpretation: Sharp peaks/troughs + a cross of the trigger line = reversal.

definition: A simple geometric pattern identifying local highs and lows.
formula: Up fractal=a bar whose H is higher than the 2 bars on each side; down fractal=a bar whose L is lower than the 2 bars on each side
interpretation: Marks micro support/resistance; commonly paired with Alligator.

definition: Short-term (3–15) and long-term (30–60) EMA clusters, observing the combined force of short/long-term traders.
formula: Short group EMA(3,5,8,10,12,15), long group EMA(30,35,40,45,50,60)
interpretation: The short cluster crossing above and diverging from the long cluster = uptrend confirmed; intertwined = ranging.

definition: An extremely low-lag, highly smooth MA.
interpretation: Turns earlier than traditional MAs; rising is bullish, falling is bearish.

definition: Annualized volatility based on historical returns.
interpretation: Compared with implied volatility (IV) to judge whether options are cheap or expensive.

definition: Auto-adjusts smoothness by market efficiency — sensitive in trends, sluggish in ranges.
interpretation: More noise-resistant in choppy markets; crossovers or turns serve as signals.

definition: A trend channel with an EMA midline + ATR bandwidth.
interpretation: Breaking above the upper band = strength; Bollinger Bands contracting inside the KC = squeeze (TTM Squeeze).

definition: Weighted momentum combining ROC across four periods.
interpretation: Crossing the signal line/crossing zero = medium-to-long-term signal.

definition: Combines volume-price with trend direction to anticipate reversals.
interpretation: Crossing above the signal line = buy; divergence + zero cross signals a medium-term reversal.

definition: Identifies reversals via the expansion/contraction of the high-low range (directionless).
interpretation: Reversal bulge: rising above 27 then falling below 26.5 = possible reversal.

definition: The absolute difference between the current price and the price n periods ago.
interpretation: >0 upward momentum, <0 downward momentum; zero crosses and divergence serve as signals.

definition: The percentage version of MACD, convenient for cross-asset comparison.
interpretation: Used like MACD, but allows comparing stocks at different price levels.

definition: Accumulate price changes on high-volume days/low-volume days respectively.
formula: Volume up updates PVI, volume down updates NVI, accumulated by close return
interpretation: NVI rising on low-volume days = "smart money" building positions (a bull-market sign).

definition: An OBV refinement, accumulating volume weighted by the close's "percentage" change.
interpretation: Moving with price = volume-price confirmation; divergence warns.

definition: A generalization of RSI, replacing "vs yesterday" with "vs n days ago".
formula: Same as RSI, but compute gains/losses from C−C₍ₜ₋m₎ then smooth
interpretation: >70 overbought, <30 oversold; smoother than RSI.

definition: The percentage change relative to the price n periods ago.
formula: ROC=(C−C n periods ago)/C n periods ago×100
interpretation: Crossing the zero line = momentum reversal; extremes signal overbought/oversold.

definition: Measures trend vigor based on "the close tending to exceed the open in uptrends".
formula: RVI=weighted(C−O)/weighted(H−L), signal line is its 4-period symmetric weighting
interpretation: Crossing above the signal line = buy; divergence warns of reversal.

definition: Compares actual travel against a random walk to judge whether a trend is real.
formula: RWI_H=(H−L n periods ago)/(ATR√n), RWI_L=(H n periods ago−L)/(ATR√n)
interpretation: RWI_H>1 = significant uptrend (non-random); both <1 = no trend.

definition: Stop And Reverse, a stop-loss/reversal point that follows the trend.
formula: SAR=SARₚᵣₑᵥ+AF×(EP−SARₚᵣₑᵥ), AF starts at 0.02 and increases 0.02 each new extreme, capped at 0.20
interpretation: Dots below the candles = uptrend (hold long), jumping above = turn short; commonly used as a trailing stop.

definition: The dispersion of price relative to its mean.
interpretation: Rising σ = increasing volatility and rising risk.

definition: A Stochastic refinement, using the close relative to the range's "midpoint".
interpretation: >+40 overbought, <−40 oversold; less noise.

definition: Wilder's recursive smoothed MA (used internally by RSI/ATR).
interpretation: Smoother with more lag, suited to medium-to-long-term.

definition: Measures trend strength by price deviation relative to a MA.
interpretation: >80 strong uptrend, <20 strong downtrend; near 50 no trend.

definition: Triple-EMA smoothed then taking the rate of change, a noise-filtered trend/momentum indicator.
interpretation: Crossing the zero line or signal line = buy; suited to medium-to-long-term.

definition: Double-EMA smoothing of momentum, then normalized.
interpretation: The zero line divides bull/bear; crossing above the signal line = buy.

definition: Combines buying pressure across short, medium and long periods to reduce false divergences.
interpretation: >70 overbought, <30 oversold; a classic bullish divergence + breakout is a buy point.

definition: Captures the start and direction of a trend via the relationship between highs and lows.
interpretation: +VI crossing above −VI = bullish, vice versa bearish; wider separation = stronger trend.

definition: Uses CMO to measure momentum and adaptively adjust EMA speed.
interpretation: Sensitive in high volatility, smooth in calm markets.

definition: Expected volatility derived from S&P 500 option implied volatility (the "fear gauge").
formula: Annualized by weighting near-/next-month SPX option IV per the CBOE method
interpretation: >30 panic, <15 calm; usually inverse to the stock index.

definition: The difference between two volume MAs (a volume MACD).
interpretation: >0 and rising = strengthening volume; confirms breakouts.

definition: Tallies the volume distribution across price levels.
formula: Volume within the range binned by price; POC=price level with the most volume; VA=value area holding about 70% of volume
interpretation: The POC and the upper/lower edges of the value area are strong support/resistance; HVN consolidates, LVN is easily traversed quickly.

definition: The speed of volume expansion/contraction.
formula: VROC=(V−V n periods ago)/V n periods ago×100
interpretation: A sudden spike = volume burst (breakout/reversal); sustained weakness = thin trading.

definition: The close's position within the recent range (a mirror of Stochastic).
interpretation: −20~0 overbought, −100~−80 oversold.

definition: Linearly weighted, with the most recent period carrying the greatest weight.
interpretation: Lag between SMA and EMA; commonly a building block for HMA and others.

definition: Filters out minor fluctuations and connects significant high/low pivots.
formula: Draws a new pivot only when price reverses ≥ a set % or point amount
interpretation: Does not predict and repaints; used to identify wave structure and draw trend lines and Fibonacci.

definition: De-lags the price then takes an EMA, nearly eliminating delay.
interpretation: Turns extremely early, needs to be paired with filter conditions.

definition: Total liabilities as a share of total assets; overall leverage.
interpretation: 40-60% is sound, >70% indicates high pressure; real estate/banks are naturally higher.

definition: Annual dividend per share relative to share price.
interpretation: >3-4% is considered high-yield; beware the "high-yield trap" caused by a sharp price decline.
Denominator of PEG; earnings growth > revenue growth indicates improving profitability.

definition: Operating cash flow minus capex; cash truly free to deploy.
interpretation: The holy grail of valuation and value investing, a DCF input; consistently positive and growing = genuine value creation.
Reflects pricing power and cost control; higher and more stable is better, consumer/software 60%+.
The most comprehensive profitability metric; one of the three DuPont factors.

definition: Share price relative to net assets per share.
formula: market cap / equity attributable to parent
interpretation: <1 trades below book; suits banks/real estate/asset-heavy firms; often used with ROE (PB=PE×ROE).

definition: Computed using the most recent 4 quarters of net income; highly timely.
interpretation: Default convention on Xueqiu/Wind; most commonly used for cross-company comparison and historical percentile.

definition: Year-over-year growth in revenue.
interpretation: The starting point of growth; distinguish volume growth / price growth / M&A.

definition: Net profit generated per unit of shareholder equity.
formula: net income attributable to parent / average equity attributable to parent (DuPont = net margin × turnover × equity multiplier)
interpretation: Long-term >15% is excellent; beware high ROE propped up by high leverage.
>2 healthy, <1 under pressure; too high (>3) means idle assets.

formula: (net income attributable to parent - preferred dividends) / weighted average shares
interpretation: The denominator of PE; watch the growth trend and dilution.
Accounts for dilution, closer to shareholders' actual earnings growth.

definition: EV relative to EBITDA; comparable across capital structures.
formula: EV/EBITDA, EBITDA = operating income + depreciation + amortization (holds when operating income ≈ EBIT; China's "operating income" includes investment income/fair-value changes, so the strict convention reverts to EBIT = net income + interest + income tax)
interpretation: First choice for M&A valuation; generally 6-12x.
>100% is excellent; long-term <60-70% warrants caution on earnings quality.

formula: stated directly on the cash flow statement
interpretation: The core engine of value generation; long-term OCF > net income is healthy.
>1 means earnings are backed by cash; consistently <1 = paper profits (a powerful tool for detecting fraud).
30-60% is balanced; >100% is unsustainable.

definition: The percentile of current PE/PB over the past N years.
formula: number of days historically below the current value / total days
interpretation: <20-30% relatively undervalued, >80% relatively overvalued; a core market-timing reference.

definition: Ratio of PE to earnings growth rate; calibrates valuation by growth.
interpretation: ≈1 fair, <1 undervalued, >1 overvalued; only applicable to stable positive growth.

definition: Share price relative to revenue per share.
formula: market cap / total revenue (TTM commonly used)
interpretation: Applies to growth firms with revenue but no profit; should be paired with gross margin.
>1 is safe; far below the current ratio indicates high inventory-liquidation risk.
Measures efficiency of all assets stripped of leverage; banks <1.5%.

definition: After-tax operating return on invested capital (debt + equity − cash).
formula: NOPAT / invested capital, NOPAT = EBIT × (1 - tax rate), invested capital = interest-bearing debt + equity - cash
interpretation: Only ROIC > WACC creates value; core to moat analysis.

formula: (net income - operating cash flow) / average total assets
interpretation: Higher means worse earnings quality (the accruals anomaly), with typically lower future returns.

formula: net income attributable to parent excluding non-recurring items / weighted shares
interpretation: Strips out one-off gains/losses, reflecting true core-business profitability.

definition: 5 weighted ratios predicting bankruptcy risk within 2 years.
formula: Z=1.2X₁+1.4X₂+3.3X₃+0.6X₄+1.0X₅ (X₁ working capital/total assets, X₂ retained earnings/total assets, X₃ EBIT/total assets, X₄ market value of equity/total liabilities, X₅ revenue/total assets)
interpretation: >2.99 safe; 1.81-2.99 gray zone; <1.81 high risk.
Used with ROA; high asset growth but declining ROA = inefficient expansion.

definition: 8 variables identifying suspected earnings manipulation.
interpretation: >−1.78 possible manipulation; <−1.78 more credible (once flagged Enron).
Reduces share count and boosts EPS; buybacks add more value when undervalued.

formula: equity attributable to parent / shares outstanding
interpretation: The denominator of PB; book value per share from a liquidation perspective.
Smooths single-year volatility; 3-year/5-year commonly used.

formula: cash paid to acquire/build fixed, intangible, and long-term assets
interpretation: Capex/depreciation >1 = expanding capacity.
The lower, the more "asset-light" and the more ample free cash flow.

formula: (operating cash flow + interest + tax) / interest expense
interpretation: A cash-based measure is more realistic; divergence from the earnings-based version warrants attention to cash quality.

formula: (cash & equivalents + trading financial assets) / current liabilities
interpretation: The strictest short-term solvency measure; >0.5 is relatively safe, too high means low cash efficiency.
>1 means cash can cover short-term debt; one of real estate's "three red lines" (≥1).

Close to or >1 is best (tax-inclusive basis; after the April 2019 VAT cut to 13%/9%/6%, goods sales ≈1.13 and blended ≈1.06-1.13; the 1.17 corresponding to the old 17% rate is outdated); a low value indicates heavy credit sales.

definition: Days to convert inventory & receivables into cash, net of payables.
interpretation: The shorter the better, and can be negative (the Dell/Amazon model) = a very strong cash advantage.

formula: stated directly on the cash flow statement
interpretation: Negative = debt repayment/dividends/buybacks (normal for mature high-quality firms).

formula: stated directly on the cash flow statement
interpretation: Usually negative during the growth phase (expansion); long-term positive may mean selling off assets to survive.
The denominator of PCF; hard to manipulate, and >EPS indicates high cash content.

formula: (revenue - cost - three expense categories - taxes & surcharges) / pre-tax income
interpretation: The higher the share, the more sustainable the earnings; low means profit is "padded" by investment/non-operating items.
The source of A-share "capitalization of reserves (bonus shares)"; high values imply potential for large bonus issues.
Working-capital efficiency; the higher the better.
<1 sound, >2 relatively high; varies widely by industry.
Sustained dividend increases ("dividend aristocrats") = sound operations; a key DDM input.
The shorter the better; a component of CCC.
10/25/50 years of dividend increases = Achiever/Aristocrat/King, a defensive screening filter.
Moderately longer is more favorable (interest-free use of supplier funds).
A core input to dividend yield and payout ratio; steady growth = high-quality blue chip.
The shorter the better; rising DSO + rising revenue = a dangerous sign of loosening credit to stimulate sales.
Strips out poorly liquid assets, more conservative; firms with high goodwill are markedly above the debt-to-asset ratio.

formula: EBIT / revenue, EBIT = net income + interest + income tax
interpretation: Strips out financing and taxes, reflecting pure operating profitability.
Strips out capital structure and depreciation policy; commonly used in asset-heavy industries.

formula: adjusted net income / (weighted shares + potential dilutive shares)
interpretation: More conservative and realistic; a large gap from basic EPS indicates serious potential dilution.

formula: (period-end - period-start) / period-start equity attributable to parent
interpretation: Internally (retention) driven is the highest quality; long-term ≈ ROE × retention ratio.
One of the three DuPont factors; the larger, the higher the leverage and the more ROE is amplified.

definition: The theoretical cost to acquire the entire company (the debt-inclusive version of market cap).
formula: market cap + interest-bearing debt + minority interest + preferred stock - cash
interpretation: The numerator of EV/EBITDA and similar multiples.

name_zh: 企业价值自由现金流比
definition: EV relative to free cash flow.
interpretation: Combines debt and cash-generation ability; one of the most rigorous cash-flow multiples.

definition: EV relative to revenue (an upgraded, debt-inclusive PS).
interpretation: Applies to loss-making/low-profit growth firms; more comprehensive than PS.
The higher, the cheaper; >5-8% is typically attractive.

formula: FCF − interest × (1 - tax rate) + net borrowing
interpretation: A direct input to equity valuation (FCFE discounting).

formula: NOPAT + depreciation & amortization − change in working capital − capex
interpretation: The standard input for whole-firm valuation (WACC discounting yields EV).
The denominator of P/FCF; growth indicates a substantive rise in per-share value.
Key for manufacturing; the higher, the fuller the capacity utilization.

definition: Relative to forecast EPS for the next year.
formula: share price / forecast EPS for the next 12 months
interpretation: More reasonable for growth stocks; below the trailing PE indicates earnings are growing.
Too high (>30-50%) hides an impairment "landmine"; an early warning for A-share "goodwill blowups".
The Novy-Marx quality factor; the higher, the "cleaner", with stronger predictive power than ROA/ROE.

formula: (current period - prior period) / prior-period gross profit
interpretation: Leads net income growth; an early signal of improving profitability.
>3 safe, <1.5 high pressure; core to bond ratings.
The higher, the more efficient the management; a sharp drop signals slow-moving goods.
Measures reliance on long-term debt; the higher, the greater the long-term solvency and interest-rate risk.

definition: The total value of all shares at the current price.
interpretation: Determines size and index weight; the numerator base of nearly all valuation multiples.
Net cash is the most sound; one of real estate's "three red lines" (≤100%).
Measures short-term solvency by real cash; >1 is relatively safe.
Strips out non-operating items, better reflecting operating quality.
The lower, the better the control; a year-over-year decline = economies of scale/improved management.
Strips out non-operating/investment income, reflecting true core-business profitability.
The lower (the longer the days), the stronger the ability to occupy supplier funds (strong bargaining power).

definition: Share price relative to operating cash flow per share.
formula: market cap / net operating cash flow
interpretation: Cash flow is hard to manipulate and avoids earnings distortion; the lower the better.

definition: The multiple of share price relative to the prior full year's EPS.
interpretation: Reflects "how many years to break even at last year's earnings"; 10-20x is common in mature industries, invalid when loss-making.

definition: 9 fundamental signals (0-9) jointly assessing financial health and improvement.
formula: 4 profitability items (ROA>0, OCF>0, ROA↑, OCF>net income) + 3 leverage/liquidity items (long-term debt ratio↓, current ratio↑, no new share issuance) + 2 efficiency items (gross margin↑, asset turnover↑)
interpretation: 8-9 strong, 0-2 weak; separates the wheat from the chaff among low-PB value stocks.
Strips out tax-rate differences, facilitating cross-region comparison.
A core growth metric for tech/pharma; high and sustained signals staying power but suppresses near-term profit.
The higher, the faster the collection and the lower the bad debt.
The "reserves on hand"; if negative, dividends cannot be paid.
High retention + high ROE = high internal growth (SGR).
Labor efficiency; suitable for cross-comparison in services/tech.
Suitable for cross-comparing capital-intensive firms; >cost of capital and above peers is excellent.

formula: ROE × retention ratio, retention ratio = 1 − payout ratio
interpretation: Actual growth persistently above SGR requires external financing.

formula: (dividends + net buybacks + net debt repayment) / market cap
interpretation: More comprehensive than dividend yield; measures the total strength of cash returns.
The denominator of PS; suitable for loss-making growth stocks.

formula: equity − goodwill − intangible assets; tangible PB = market cap / TBV
interpretation: The most conservative net-asset basis; suitable for liquidation value/bank valuation.

definition: The ratio of market value to the replacement cost of assets.
interpretation: >1 above replacement cost (incentive to expand), <1 undervalued (a potential takeover target).
One of the three DuPont factors; high for retail, low for utilities.

formula: (period-end - period-start share price + dividends over the period) / period-start share price
interpretation: An investor's true total return; should be compared against the benchmark index TSR.
The higher, the more sales supported per unit of working capital; too high may signal insufficient funds.

definition: The sensitivity of a stock's volatility relative to the broad market (systematic risk).
interpretation: =1 in sync, >1 aggressive, <1 defensive, <0 inverse; a key CAPM input.

definition: Number of unique addresses that sent or received at least once in the period.
interpretation: Core network-usage gauge; price up while addresses fall = weakening trend (price-volume divergence).

definition: Net coins moving onto exchanges (inflow minus outflow).
interpretation: Sustained positive = rising sell pressure (bearish); sustained negative = bullish.

definition: Total coins held in exchange wallets.
interpretation: Sustained decline = shrinking sellable supply (structurally bullish); a multi-year decline is one of the key bull-market fundamentals.

definition: Network-wide hashes per second (mining commitment / security).
interpretation: New highs = miners bullish / more secure; sharp drop = miner capitulation (possible cycle bottom).

definition: Deviation of current market cap from the market-wide average cost basis (aggregate unrealized profit multiple).
interpretation: >3.5–4 overvalued top; <1 deeply undervalued.

definition: Standard-deviation-normalized MVRV.
interpretation: >7 historic cycle top; ≈0 or turning negative (green zone) historic cycle bottom (Z fell below 0 multiple times at the 2015/2018/2022 major bottoms; one of the most-used top/bottom timing tools).

definition: Market-wide unrealized profit minus unrealized loss as a share of market cap.
interpretation: >0.75 euphoria (top); <0 capitulation (bottom); intermediate zones split into belief/optimism/hope.

definition: Market-wide average cost basis per coin.
interpretation: Key bull-bear divide; a break below = average holder underwater (deep bear market).

definition: Sale price / cost price of spent coins; the day's aggregate on-chain profit multiple.
formula: price at spend / price at creation (aggregated)
interpretation: >1 aggregate profit, <1 aggregate loss; in bull markets pullbacks to 1 bounce, in bear markets 1 acts as resistance.

definition: SOPR excluding outputs younger than 1 hour.
interpretation: Pullback to 1 holding = healthy bull market.

definition: Market-wide accumulation/distribution (0–1).
interpretation: Near 1 = large entities aggressively accumulating (bullish); near 0 = distribution.

definition: Intensity of old-coin movement.
interpretation: Spike = old holders (smart money) moving/selling (warning).

definition: The premier short-term gauge of US institutional buying power.
formula: Coinbase Pro (BTC/USD) price − Binance (BTC/USDT) price (or its percentage)
interpretation: Positive premium = US institutions/ETFs buying actively (bullish); negative premium = US sell pressure or Asia-led; often corroborates spot ETF net inflows.

definition: Only coins ≥155 days old.
interpretation: Very high = long-term holders taking large profits (top); <1 = capitulation (bottom).

definition: Coins ≥155 days old (strong hands).
interpretation: Sustained rise = accumulation (bullish bottom); peaks and rolls over ahead of the top.

definition: The network's "P/E ratio."
formula: market cap / daily on-chain transfer volume (USD)
interpretation: High = valuation expensive relative to usage; low = undervalued.

formula: count of addresses appearing for the first time in the period
interpretation: Rising = adoption expanding (bull-market demand); peaking and rolling over = incremental capital drying up.

formula: count of entities with balance ≥1,000 BTC
interpretation: Increasing = large capital bullish; whale moves often lead price.

definition: Smoothed, refined NVT, more sensitive to overheating.
interpretation: A high-level rollover signals a top; low levels mark a value zone.

definition: Share of coins whose current price exceeds the price at their last move.
interpretation: Near 95–100% overheated top; <40–50% often corresponds to a bottom.

definition: Supply-side overheating/undervaluation.
formula: daily miner issuance revenue (USD) / its 365-day average
interpretation: >4 cycle top; <0.5 historic buy zone.

definition: Sum of each coin valued at its last-moved price (cumulative capital / total cost basis).
interpretation: Denominator foundation for MVRV/NUPL/RHODL; rising = capital inflow.

definition: BTC market cap relative to total stablecoin market cap.
formula: BTC market cap / total stablecoin market cap
interpretation: Low = strong potential buying power (bullish); high = buying power depleted (bearish).

definition: Aggregate unrealized profit of short-term (<155 days) vs long-term (≥155 days) holders, respectively.
formula: STH-MVRV = STH market cap / STH realized cap (LTH likewise)
interpretation: STH-MVRV <1 short-term holders aggregate underwater (often a bottom/strong support), >1.2–1.5 short-term overheated; LTH-MVRV very high = long-term holders taking large profits (top distribution); a practical core of the bull-bear framework.

definition: Only coins <155 days old.
interpretation: Reflects newcomer sentiment, highly volatile.

definition: Coins <155 days old (new entrants).
interpretation: The STH cost basis is a key bull-bear support/resistance.
Share at new highs = strong accumulation (late bear/early bull); rapid drop = old coins reviving into distribution (top warning).

definition: 200-week moving average overlaid with color-coded monthly returns.
interpretation: Clustered red dots = top; near/below the 200WMA = historic buy zone.

formula: distinct active entities in the period
interpretation: More robust than address count against "one user, many addresses" distortion; divergences likewise serve as a warning.

formula: count of active validators (ETH: 32 ETH per validator)
interpretation: Growth = improving security and decentralization; entry/exit queues reflect staking demand.

definition: Ratio of active market cap to investor market cap (deviation from the true market average price).
formula: active market cap / investor market cap, long-run mean ≈1
interpretation: >1 hot, <1 cold, ≈1 fair value.

formula: count of addresses with balance ≥ threshold (0.1/1/10/100/1k BTC)
interpretation: ≥1 BTC ("Wholecoiner") reflects retail accumulation; larger bands reflect whale increases/decreases.

definition: NUPL excluding dormant/lost coins.
formula: ((market cap − dormant supply × price) − adjusted realized cap) / market cap
interpretation: More reliable bottom signal.

name_zh: 平均/中位花费产出寿命
formula: ASOL = Σ lifespan / output count; MSOL = median(lifespan)
interpretation: High = old outputs moving (distribution); MSOL resists outliers to judge how broad-based it is.

formula: total fees / tx count; median(per-tx fee)
interpretation: High = congestion; the median better represents the typical user experience.

definition: A fair-value floor that nets out holding time.
formula: Realized Price − Transferred Price (Transferred Price = lifetime value-weighted price derived from coin-days destroyed)
interpretation: Pullbacks near this level are mostly deep-bear buying opportunities.

formula: 1 if the day's CDD exceeds its long-run average, else 0
interpretation: Sustained high = distribution phase; sustained low = accumulation phase.
Lengthening = hash rate dropped sharply (before difficulty adjusts).
Near the cap = demand saturated, fees rise; low = ample block space.

formula: Bridge TVL = value locked in bridge contracts; netflow = inflow − outflow
interpretation: Net inflow to a chain = that ecosystem attracting capital; bridges are also a security attack surface.
Abnormal spike = fork risk / attack signs; low = robust consensus.
The base denominator for market cap, realized price, etc.
An overly high single-client share = systemic bug risk; diversification is more robust.

definition: Supply layered by coin age to observe supply structure.
interpretation: Older bands swelling = accumulation phase; younger bands swelling = distribution phase.
Stablecoin benchmark = on-chain low-risk rate; abnormally high APY comes with high risk.

definition: A pricing floor for identifying major cycle bottoms.
interpretation: Delta Price often acts as historic bottom support.

formula: cumulative balance of derivative exchange addresses
interpretation: Compared with spot balances to distinguish capital migrating between spot vs leverage.

formula: Σ(volume per DEX); DEX/CEX = DEX volume / CEX volume
interpretation: Rising = trading migrating on-chain, increasing DeFi adoption.

formula: protocol adjusts roughly every 2016 blocks to block speed
interpretation: Upward adjustment = hash rate flooding in (bullish); consecutive downward adjustments = miners leaving (capitulation, common at bottoms).

definition: Resources required to sustain an attack.
formula: protocol adjusts roughly every 2016 blocks to block speed
interpretation: Steady rise = growing security budget; consecutive large drops = risk of miners withdrawing.
Compression/inversion = miner capitulation (historic buy zone); diverging upward = healthy bull market.

definition: Average coin-days carried per spent coin.
formula: day's CDD / day's transferred coin volume
interpretation: Rising = old coins moving (distribution/top); falling = new-coin turnover (accumulation).

definition: Computed after merging an entity's multiple addresses, removing self-transfer noise.
formula: cluster to identify entities, then aggregate at the entity level
interpretation: Closer to true economic activity.

formula: Σ(Gas Used × Base Fee), net issuance = issuance − burn
interpretation: Burn > issuance = deflationary (bullish fundamentals).
Rising = more locked up, less circulating (bullish supply side).
>0.85 = whales dominate inflows (may signal large sell-offs / high volatility).

name_zh: 交易所充值笔数/地址数
formula: count of transactions / distinct addresses flowing into exchanges
interpretation: Surge = retail sell pressure rising (short-term top sentiment corroboration).

formula: coin volume flowing into exchange addresses
interpretation: Surge = potential sell pressure (bearish).

formula: coin volume flowing out of exchanges
interpretation: Surge = withdrawals to self-custody / reluctance to sell (bullish).

formula: total value / count of exchange-related transfers (and median)
interpretation: Rising average size = institutions/whales dominate; falling = more retail-driven.

definition: True tradable supply after excluding the long-term non-sellable portion.
interpretation: The smaller it is, the more easily price is pushed by marginal capital.
Lower = fees can more independently sustain security (more sustainable).

formula: tx fee = Gas Used × (Base Fee + Priority Fee)
interpretation: High = strong on-chain demand (active DeFi/NFT); subdued = quiet.

definition: Measures the cost of a 51% attack.
interpretation: Higher = higher attack cost, more secure; a collapse = dual security and sentiment risk.

definition: Identifies miner capitulation and recovery.
formula: 30-day hash rate MA crossing below 60-day = capitulation; re-cross above + price momentum = buy
interpretation: The buy signal has a high historical win rate, often at bottoms.

definition: Daily revenue per unit of hash rate ($/PH/s/day).
formula: daily total miner revenue (USD) / network hash rate
interpretation: Falling squeezes miner profits, or triggers shutdowns/capitulation.

definition: Supply stacked into buckets by "age since last moved."
interpretation: Old coins (warm colors) swelling = mature accumulation (near bottom / early bull); new coins (cool colors) swelling = heavy turnover (near top).

formula: group by entity balance band and track count and net position change
interpretation: Comparing accumulation/distribution across cohorts reveals market structure.
Too high = whale manipulation / dump risk; falling = healthier.

definition: Coins held by entities that rarely spend.
formula: liquidity L = cumulative outflow / cumulative inflow, L ≲0.25 is illiquid
interpretation: Rising = shrinking sellable supply (bullish supply side).

definition: Secondary-market average cost basis excluding miners' cost.
interpretation: A bear-market support reference; with Delta/Balanced it forms the "bottom price band."
Rising = reluctance to sell, tightening supply (bullish).

definition: IntoTheBlock's profit/loss distribution of holdings by price level.
formula: aggregate profit (ITM) / loss (OTM) by address cost basis
interpretation: Dense cost zones = strong support/resistance; heavy underwater supply above = strong rebound resistance.
Rising = increasing potential sell pressure.

definition: Macro HODL vs sell-off tendency.
interpretation: Rising = distribution (bearish); falling = accumulation (bullish).

name_zh: 流动性质押衍生品占比
formula: LST staked amount / total staked amount
interpretation: An overly high single share (e.g., Lido) = centralization/governance risk; LSTs are a foundational DeFi collateral.

definition: Price as a multiple of the 200-day moving average.
interpretation: >2.4 overheated; <1 historic buy zone.

formula: protocol token market cap / TVL (also FDV/TVL)
interpretation: <1 possibly undervalued; >>1 possibly overvalued.

definition: Measures overheating relative to cumulative miner investment.
interpretation: High level (red zone) = top, low level (green zone) = undervalued.
Rising = more large/institutional activity; falling = more retail-driven.

formula: average time from entry to first confirmation (alongside mempool size)
interpretation: Congestion = demand exceeds throughput (fees rise); clear = healthy.
Positive = net accumulation (bullish); negative = net selling (bearish).

formula: coin volume sent out of miner addresses (watch the portion going to exchanges)
interpretation: Miner→exchange surge = sell pressure (bearish).
Rising = accumulation (bullish); sustained decline = distribution (under pressure).
Basis for miner sell pressure and the security budget; post-halving fees must make up the difference long-term.

definition: Miner selling relative to historical intensity.
interpretation: >2 heavy selling (bearish); <0 accumulation (bullish).
A core input to valuation ratios (NVT/MVRV).

formula: Floor = lowest listing; Volume = traded value
interpretation: Reflects speculative heat, correlated with the liquidity cycle.

formula: count of reachable nodes detected by network scan
interpretation: More and more dispersed = more decentralized/healthy.
Long-term rise = structural adoption growth; new highs = healthy fundamentals.

definition: Net capital inflow/outflow.
interpretation: Deeply positive = net profit-taking exits (top); deeply negative = capitulation (bottom).

formula: sum of collateral value liquidated in the period
interpretation: Surge = deleveraging/cascade (sharp drop); peaks sometimes correspond to a short-term bottom.

formula: P/S = market cap / annualized revenue; P/F = market cap / annualized fees
interpretation: Low multiples + real growth = potentially undervalued; high multiples need high growth to justify.
Near 100% = broad-based profit (top sell pressure); very low = broad-based loss (bottom).

definition: Moving-average crossover that captures cycle tops.
formula: 111DMA crossing above 2×350DMA emits a top signal
interpretation: Has flagged cycle tops precisely multiple times historically (small sample).

formula: Top-N hash rate (or staking share) / network total
interpretation: A single entity near 51% or an outsized staking provider share = security risk (e.g., the Lido share controversy).

formula: Fees = total fees paid by users; Revenue = portion accruing to the protocol
interpretation: Real revenue is a core DeFi fundamental; P/F and P/S are used for valuation.

definition: HODL Waves weighted by realized value (wealth share).
interpretation: A surge in young-age wealth share = new capital dominating, overheated.

definition: USD loss locked in by selling at a loss that day.
formula: Σ(cost price − sale price) × amount (sale < cost)
interpretation: Surge = panic capitulation (often corresponds to a bottom).

definition: USD profit locked in by selling at a profit that day.
formula: Σ(sale price − cost price) × amount (sale > cost)
interpretation: Surge = large-scale profit-taking (top).

formula: on the entity-adjusted basis, further remove internal consolidation / intra-exchange transfers
interpretation: Assesses true settlement demand, avoiding being misled by inflated volume.

definition: Long-term holders' confidence vs price risk/reward.
formula: price / HODL Bank (HODL Bank built from the opportunity cost of cumulative CDD)
interpretation: Low level (green zone) historic buy point; high level (red zone) cycle top.

definition: Wealth share of new vs old holders.
formula: (1-week RCap band / 1–2-year RCap band) × market age (days)
interpretation: High red zone = top, low level = bottom.
Very high = top, very low = bottom capitulation.

definition: Prices via scarcity (stock / annual flow).
formula: Stock/Flow, power-law regression mapped to price
interpretation: Halvings double S2F; large deviations and much debate in recent years, best used as a framework.
Cross-cycle comparable intensity of old-coin activity.
A sending surge may accompany sell-offs; receiving expansion often represents distribution/accumulation.

definition: Nansen's label tracking of historically outperforming wallets.
formula: tag by historical profit/behavior and aggregate net flow
interpretation: Net inflow often leads price; an exit = warning.

definition: Coins spent that day bucketed by age.
interpretation: A sudden jump in the >1y bucket = old holders distributing (warning).

formula: SSR Bollinger Band normalized (SSR − mean) / σ
interpretation: Touching the lower band = buying power abundant (bullish); touching the upper band = depleted (bearish).
Large inflows = potential buying power waiting (bullish).

formula: cumulative stablecoins held in exchange addresses
interpretation: Rising = more buy-side dry powder (bullish setup).
Expansion = new fiat buying power entering (macro bullish); contraction = liquidity leaving.

formula: stablecoin market cap / total crypto market cap; depeg = deviation from $1
interpretation: High share = more risk-off/sidelined; a severe depeg = credit-risk event (UST/USDC).
Higher = more locked up, less sellable (bullish), and it also affects liquidity.
The on-chain "risk-free rate"; too low reduces appeal.

definition: Unrealized profit share grouped by holding period.
formula: per group (group market cap − group realized value) / group market cap
interpretation: STH extremes are often contrarian; LTH is typically elevated ahead of a top.

definition: Supply bucketed by address/entity balance size.
interpretation: Large-balance buckets rising = whale accumulation/concentration; small-balance buckets rising = retail entering.

definition: Cumulative sum of all historical mining revenue valued at the price at the time (total network-security investment).
formula: Σ(daily new issuance × price that day)
interpretation: Often used as a denominator to build ratios.

definition: The true average cost basis of active investors, excluding dormant/lost coins.
interpretation: The bull-bear "centerline"; above = bullish, below = bearish.
Surge = rising leverage demand; high leverage + a decline easily triggers cascading liquidations.

formula: sum of all transaction fees in the period
interpretation: Spike = strong block-space demand (congestion/high activity); the network's "revenue."

formula: sum of tokens locked in staking contracts / by validators
interpretation: Rising = long-term bullishness and less circulating (tightening supply).
Near the theoretical limit = congestion; high-throughput chains often compare performance by this.

formula: total confirmed transactions in the period
interpretation: Growth = rising adoption; divergence serves as a trend warning.

formula: sum of coin volume moved by all transactions in the period
interpretation: Raw volume is noisy and easily overstated; a sustained volume surge confirming a breakout is more reliable.

formula: coin volume moved between distinct entities (USD, excluding self-transfers)
interpretation: Closer to true throughput; a more reliable denominator for NVT/NVTS.

definition: USD value of assets locked in protocol/chain contracts.
interpretation: Rising = capital inflow/confidence; beware TVL inflated by incentives.
Rising pushes rates up; near 100% = liquidity tightness / run risk.
Growth = increasing usage/accumulation; the share of UTXOs in profit is also a sentiment indicator.

definition: A USD-weighted, normalized version of CDD.
interpretation: High = old holders distributing at high prices (top); low = bottom.

formula: annualized transfer volume / average circulating supply (or market cap)
interpretation: Rising = more used for transacting; falling accompanies accumulation (stronger HODLing).

definition: Expected 30-day annualized volatility implied by SPX options.
formula: Variance-weighted interpolation of near-term + next-term SPX options to 30 days, then annualized
interpretation: <15 complacency, >30 fear, >40 extreme (often marks bottoms); usually negatively correlated with equity indices.

definition: US equity sentiment, 0-100.
formula: Equal weight of 7 inputs - momentum/strength/breadth/Put-Call/VIX(inverse)/safe-haven demand/junk-bond demand
interpretation: Used for contrarian timing of US equities.

definition: 0-100 composite gauge of crypto sentiment.
formula: Alternative.me six-factor weighting - volatility 25% + momentum/volume 25% + social 15% + surveys 15% + BTC dominance 10% + Google Trends 10% (totals 100%; the "Surveys" component is currently suspended)
interpretation: 0-24 extreme fear (often a buy), 75-100 extreme greed (caution); typically contrarian.

definition: Periodic funding payment between perpetual longs and shorts, anchoring the perpetual to spot.
formula: Funding=Notional×[Premium+clamp(Interest−Premium, −0.05%, +0.05%)] (Binance standard, clamp bounds ±0.05%), settled every 8h, annualized≈rate×3×365
interpretation: Extremely high positive funding = crowded/overheated longs (contrarian bearish); deeply negative = crowded shorts (often precedes a bounce).

definition: Value of leveraged positions force-closed.
formula: Long liquidations + short liquidations per unit time (notional USD)
interpretation: Large long liquidations often accompany sharp drops (possible short-term bottom); large short liquidations = short-squeeze top; liquidation clusters = sentiment extremes.

formula: Taker buy/sell ratio / top-trader position ratio / top-trader account ratio = long/short
interpretation: Retail extremely long → contrarian bearish; the top-trader long/short ratio offers better directional guidance.

definition: Total open interest (capital participation / leverage size).
formula: All outstanding contracts (count or notional USD)
interpretation: Price up + OI up = new longs entering (healthy); price up + OI down = short covering (weak momentum); spikes often precede sharp volatility.
Sustained net inflows = new demand (bullish); large net outflows = capital leaving.

formula: Same as above, aggregated across ETH ETFs
interpretation: Same as BTC ETF; watch ETH's institutional preference relative to BTC.

definition: Retail investors' bullish/neutral/bearish view on the next 6 months.
interpretation: Contrarian indicator; extremely high Bull% (>50%) often marks interim tops; prefer the 8-week average.

formula: Share of Top 50 outperforming BTC over the past 90 days; ≥75% means altcoin season
interpretation: 0-25 Bitcoin season, 75-100 altcoin season.
High = high cash-and-carry yield, but also signals overheated long leverage.

formula: futures price − spot price (>0 premium/contango, <0 discount/backwardation)
interpretation: Widening positive basis = bullish optimism; backwardation often signals fear/short squeeze (contrarian).

formula: BTC market cap / total crypto market cap
interpretation: Rising = risk-off / capital rotating back into BTC (altcoins weak); falling = capital broadening out (potential altcoin season).

definition: CFTC weekly positioning by trader category.
formula: Split into commercial/non-commercial/non-reportable, net position = long − short (released Friday for Tuesday's data)
interpretation: Non-commercial net long/short at multi-year extremes = crowded trade (contrarian); covers equity indices/gold/crude/FX.

definition: Expected future volatility implied by option prices.
formula: Solve the pricing model with the market price as input, annualized
interpretation: High = expensive options / expectation of large moves (pre-event); compare with realized volatility to judge cheap/expensive.

definition: Where current IV sits within its 1-year range (0–100).
interpretation: >50 relatively expensive (seller strategies), <20 relatively cheap (buyer strategies).
Net inflow + price up = capital-driven; price up but net outflow = suspected distribution into strength.

formula: Margin financing balance + securities-lending balance (US equities: see FINRA)
interpretation: Rapidly rising margin = overheated leverage; sharp declines = deleveraging/panic.

formula: Shanghai Connect + Shenzhen Connect net buying for the day
interpretation: Sustained net inflows = foreign capital bullish on A-shares (note: real-time disclosure rules have been revised).

formula: Put volume / Call volume (volume or OI)
interpretation: Contrarian; extremely high = fear (bottom), extremely low = complacency (top).

formula: Put volume (or OI) / Call volume (or OI)
interpretation: Contrarian; extremely high (>1.0-1.2) excessive fear (bottom), extremely low (<0.7) excessive optimism.

formula: Change in total on-chain stablecoin market cap, net inflows to exchanges
interpretation: Supply expansion + inflows to exchanges = ample buying power (bullish).
Positive = downside protection more expensive (bearish); negative (inverted) = Calls more expensive (squeeze/extremely bullish).

formula: Count advances/declines, look at the difference or ratio
interpretation: Index up but more decliners = breadth divergence (weak).

formula: Number of stocks above their 200-day MA / total
interpretation: Long bull markets often hold high readings; falling below 50% = weakening long-term trend.

formula: Number of stocks above their 50-day MA / total
interpretation: >70-80% overbought, <20% oversold.
Index makes a new high but the AD Line does not = top divergence (deteriorating breadth).
Extremely high overbought, extremely low oversold; smooth over several days for a read.

formula: (total market cap − BTC[−ETH/stablecoins])/total market cap (TradingView TOTAL2/TOTAL3)
interpretation: Complementary to BTC.D; rising = altcoins broadly strengthening.

formula: IV of the Call/Put nearest at-the-money (average/interpolated)
interpretation: Baseline for term-structure and smile analysis; drops sharply (IV crush) after events.

formula: Nominal Treasury yield − same-maturity TIPS yield
interpretation: Rising often benefits inflation-hedge assets (gold/some crypto narratives); influences the Fed's path.
<2 contrarian buy, >8 contrarian sell.

formula: Number of stocks on P&F buy signals / total
interpretation: >70% overbought zone, <30% oversold zone.
Usually negatively correlated (strong USD → weak BTC); a strengthening correlation signals macro (liquidity) is in control.
Rising positive correlation = BTC seen as safe-haven/inflation hedge; the correlation is highly variable.
High positive correlation = treated as a high-beta risk asset; a pullback raises the odds of an independent move.

name_zh: 加密与美债收益率关联
interpretation: Rising real rates (tightening) pressure high-valuation growth stocks and crypto.
>2-3 leans greedy, <0.5-1 leans fearful.

formula: Strike with the largest Call Gamma/OI = Call Wall; the largest Put's = Put Wall
interpretation: Under positive Gamma, price is often "pinned" between the two walls; breaking a wall triggers acceleration.

definition: Pricing of SPX left-tail (crash) risk.
formula: Skewness computed from out-of-the-money options, mapped to 100-150
interpretation: High = expensive crash-protection premium; high SKEW + low VIX = hidden risk.

formula: CME contract OI/volume; gap = price vacuum between Friday close and Sunday open
interpretation: Rising CME OI share = increasing institutional participation; price often "fills the CME gap."
Rising = growth expectations/risk-on (often moves with bond yields); falling = risk-off.
Price makes a new high but CVD does not = top divergence.
Gauges dealers' directional positioning and potential buy/sell pressure.

formula: Share of futures retail traders that are bullish, 0-100%
interpretation: >90% extreme optimism (bearish), <10% extreme pessimism (bullish); a classic contrarian read.

definition: Crypto VIX (BTC/ETH 30-day expected annualized volatility).
interpretation: Spikes = surging uncertainty; extremely high often marks bottom panic.

formula: Geometric weighting against 6 currencies; check rolling correlation for relationships
interpretation: A strengthening DXY is usually bearish for risk assets (negative correlation).

formula: Share change × NAV, aggregated by sector
interpretation: Inflows to growth/tech = rising risk appetite; inflows to defensives = risk-off (sector rotation).
Strengthening = early signal of a broad altcoin move; weakening = preference for BTC.
Heavy inflows = potential selling pressure (bearish); outflows to cold wallets = accumulation/hodling (bullish).
Easing (falling) = ample liquidity (bullish for risk assets); tightening (rising) = headwind; a core gauge of macro liquidity.

definition: Market makers' aggregate net Gamma, affecting hedging and volatility.
interpretation: Positive Gamma = buy-low/sell-high suppresses volatility; negative Gamma = chase up/dump down amplifies volatility; the Gamma Flip is the key switch.
Retail search peaks often coincide with mania/tops; quiet readings = end of a bear market.

formula: (market price − NAV per share)/NAV per share
interpretation: Large premium = mania, deep discount = extreme pessimism; converges after ETF conversion.

formula: Growth/Value = growth index / value index (same for high/low beta)
interpretation: Growth/high-beta outperforming = risk-on; value/low-volatility outperforming = risk-off.
Widening = risk-off/recession fears (bearish for equities); narrowing = recovering risk appetite; a macro risk thermometer.
>3 overheated, <1 overly pessimistic; a contrarian read.

formula: Aggregated quarterly 13F filings; corporate holdings from announcements
interpretation: Institutions/corporates adding = long-term confidence; concentrated selling warrants caution.

formula: Days over the past ~252 trading days with IV below the current level / total trading days × 100
interpretation: More robust to extremes than IVR; >80% historically elevated.

formula: Count limit-up/limit-down stocks, consecutive-limit streak height, seal rate
interpretation: Surging limit-ups + rising streaks = euphoric sentiment (overheated); a wave of limit-downs = panic (short-term contrarian).

formula: Estimate and color-code cumulative liquidation amounts at each price level by leverage and position distribution
interpretation: Dense liquidation zones act as price "magnets," tending to reverse after sweeping liquidity.

formula: Number of net-long accounts / number of net-short accounts
interpretation: Retail extremely long is often contrarian bearish; more effective when cross-checked against the top-trader ratio.
Extremely long-skewed liquidations = panic sell-off (contrarian bullish).

definition: The strike at which option buyers suffer the greatest aggregate loss at expiry.
formula: The strike minimizing total Call + Put intrinsic value
interpretation: Approaching expiry, price is often "magnetized" toward Max Pain.

name_zh: 麦克莱伦成交量振荡器
formula: Similarly based on ratio-adjusted net volume = EMA19 − EMA39 of (up volume − down volume)/(up volume + down volume) (not raw net volume)
interpretation: Volume-weighted breadth momentum; positive/negative = buy/sell pressure dominant.
Record highs = large cash on the sidelines (buying reserve/risk-off); declines accompany capital rotating back into risk assets.

formula: Active managers' average equity exposure (−200% to +200%)
interpretation: 100%+ fully invested (overheated), low/negative = defensive; contrarian.

formula: Net new highs = new highs − new lows; also see the High-Low Index
interpretation: Heavy new lows = internal deterioration; index makes a new high but new-high count shrinks = divergence.

formula: First compute ratio-adjusted net advances RANA=(advances − declines)/(advances + declines), then EMA19(RANA) − EMA39(RANA) (StockCharts standard; taking the EMA directly on raw net issues is an early simplified version that distorts as constituent count grows)
interpretation: >0 bullish, <0 bearish; extreme values overbought/oversold, divergences common.

formula: Prior value + the day's McClellan Oscillator
interpretation: Crossing zero = medium-term bull/bear shift; direction matters more than the absolute level.
Combine with price into four quadrants to identify new longs/new shorts/long covering/short covering.

formula: Notional contract OI / underlying total market cap
interpretation: Elevated = crowded leverage, high risk of wick liquidations.

formula: Σ(each exchange's rate × each exchange's OI)/ΣOI
interpretation: More robust than a single exchange; broadly high positive = market-wide overheated longs.
Large Call OI = resistance (Call Wall), large Put OI = support (Put Wall).
Sustained positive = longs in control; extreme values signal funding and price mean-reversion pressure.

name_zh: 已实现市值净流入(链上)
interpretation: Expansion = new capital entering (bullish); contraction = capital leaving.

formula: x-axis JdK RS-Ratio, y-axis RS-Momentum, split into four quadrants
interpretation: Clockwise rotation (Leading → Weakening → Lagging → Improving), for offensive/defensive allocation.

definition: Strength of a stock/sector relative to a benchmark (not RSI).
interpretation: Rising RS = leading stocks/strong sectors; market leaders often have RS >80.

definition: Contrasts retail (open) vs institutional (close) intraday flows.
formula: Prior SMFI − first 30-minute move + last 1-hour move
interpretation: Divergence between the close (institutions) and the open (retail) = a turning-point warning.

formula: NLP sentiment score + mention volume/author count
interpretation: Surging discussion + excessive optimism = local top; social quiet + stabilizing = bottom signal.

formula: Shanghai/Shenzhen-HK Stock Connect net buying for the day
interpretation: Heavy inflows = mainland capital favoring HK equities (tech/dividend).

formula: Rolling correlation of equity index and Treasury prices (alongside VIX/credit spreads)
interpretation: Stocks down/bonds up (negative) = effective hedge; stocks and bonds both down (positive, high-inflation periods) = hedge fails / liquidity crisis.

formula: Taker buy/sell ratio = aggressive buys / aggressive sells
interpretation: Aggressive buying + price up = strong momentum; aggressive buying but stalling = suspected distribution.

formula: x-axis expiry, y-axis ATM IV (normally upward-sloping contango)
interpretation: Inversion (near-term > far-term) = short-term fear (often a sell-off period/bottom).
>1 leans bearish, <1 leans bullish; intraday >2-3 often signals panic selling (contrarian bullish).

formula: Up-stock volume / down-stock volume; 9:1 days are called selling/buying climaxes
interpretation: Sustained heavy up-volume = institutions entering (Follow-Through Day); 90% down-volume = panic.

formula: Stablecoin market cap / total crypto market cap
interpretation: Rising = flight to stablecoins (often a price bottom); falling = capital entering.

formula: Vanna=∂Delta/∂IV; Charm=∂Delta/∂time, multiplied by OI and aggregated
interpretation: Large expiries + falling IV often drive a "drift higher" (US equity OpEx weeks).

formula: Derived from VIX options using the VIX methodology
interpretation: High VVIX + low VIX often foreshadows a pickup in volatility.

formula: Plot the curve with x-axis strike (or Delta) and y-axis IV
interpretation: Left skew (Put IV > Call IV) = greater fear of downside; steepening = rising tail-risk concern.

formula: IV − realized volatility RV (mostly positive over the long run)
interpretation: IV well above RV = options relatively expensive (favors sellers); inversion (RV > IV) often accompanies violent moves.
Extremely positive = excessive optimism (contrarian bearish); extremely negative + price holding = potential bounce.

definition: Option price sensitivity to the underlying price (1st order).
interpretation: Approximates the equivalent underlying share count; |Delta| ≈ probability of finishing in-the-money.

definition: Sensitivity of Delta to the underlying price (2nd order).
interpretation: Greatest at-the-money and near expiry; high Gamma = frequent hedging, amplifying/suppressing volatility (see GEX).

definition: Time decay of an option's value.
interpretation: Decays fastest near expiry/at-the-money; sellers collect Theta, buyers pay Theta.

definition: Option price sensitivity to implied volatility.
interpretation: Vega is large for long-dated/at-the-money options; long Vega = a bet on rising IV.

definition: Option price sensitivity to interest rates.
interpretation: Small impact on short-dated options, significant for LEAPS; rising rates benefit Calls.
